name: Thrift Poly
explanation: A lovable lo-fi eight-voice paraphonic synthesizer in the budget-classic tradition. Each voice is a DCO blending saw and square with a square sub an octave down, but all voices share ONE resonant low-pass filter and ONE envelope — so sweeping the Cutoff makes whole chords gurgle and breathe together, the signature paraphonic behaviour. A built-in stereo chorus adds width and a touch of wobble, while the gritty soft-clipped output keeps things charming rather than hi-fi. Controls: Cutoff, Resonance, Env Amount, Sub, Chorus, Release and Level.
